body.title: cc_queue.py claim — add --as <worker> flag for explicit claim-worker selection
body.result: Shipped commit bc3cbfd5. cc_queue.py claim now accepts --as <worker> flag (default 'igor' preserves cert_worker_freeze semantics for pe_chain). 4 subprocess behavior tests replace the old source-line literal assertions. Suite 4325 → 4326. Workaround that hit twice today (direct queue.json edit) is no longer needed.
body.description:
Today's session hit the cmd_claim asymmetric check twice: pe_chain expects 'only igor can claim worker=igor' (cert-freeze design — load-bearing), but CC manual claims of worker=claude tickets are also rejected by the same coarse gate. Workaround in this session was direct python edit of queue.json — fragile and bypasses logging.

**Fix:** add `--as <worker>` flag to claim. Defaults to 'igor' (preserves cert_worker_freeze semantics for pe_chain calls without the flag). CC manual claims pass `--as claude` to claim claude-worker tickets.

**Affected files:** lab/claudecode/cc_queue.py (cmd_claim signature + flag parse + check); tests/test_cc_queue_claim_worker.py (replace source-line literal assertions with subprocess-driven behavior tests).

**Design rules:** preserve cert-freeze semantics (default worker remains igor); test-actual-behavior over source-line-pattern.

**Scope boundary:** IN — flag, check, behavior tests. OUT — broader pe_chain/cert-freeze redesign; queue.json drift fix (separate ticket T-cc-queue-write-race).

**Test plan:** subprocess run of `cc_queue.py claim T-x` against fixture queue: (a) worker=igor + no flag → success (legacy pe_chain path), (b) worker=claude + no flag → reject (cert freeze still blocks Igor), (c) worker=claude + --as claude → success (new CC manual path), (d) worker=igor + --as claude → reject (cross-worker claim still blocked).
